ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: I believe that I have the ability to complete college.
I have goals that I want to achieve by being in college.
I have high academic expectations of myself.
I believe it is possible for me to make good grades.
I turn in assignments only partially completed.
I doubt that I can make the effort to finish college.
I am determined to do what it will take in order to succeed with my goals.
I do not turn in assignments.
My family cares how I do academically.
Family members criticize me because I am not a great student.
I am aware of the assignments that are due in the next week.
Gaining knowledge is important to me.
I question why I need a degree for the career I want to pursue.
People in my community value a college education.
